phase_name: Post-v4.7 milestone close + comprehensive code audit

completed_tasks.name: Phase 54 closeout — Series 22.5 in UAT-SERIES.md, Organisation→Organization spelling fix, dashboard rebuild, Obsidian Phase-54 note + UAT-Series sync
Verified zero outstanding HUMAN-UAT in v4.7 (52/53/54/55/56/56.1 all complete or passed)
Investigated gsd-audit-fix — confirmed wrong tool (UAT-debt pipeline, not code review)
Whole-codebase audit: 6 parallel gsd-code-reviewer agents at deep depth across 116 files
AUDIT-SUMMARY.md synthesis with proposed v4.8 Wave A/B phase decomposition

remaining_tasks.name: Triage top 15 blockers with stakeholder lens (enterprise-first per memory)
Decide v4.8 Wave A / Wave B split — accept proposal or revise
Run /gsd-new-milestone v4.8 with audit findings as input
Update HORIZON.md with rationale-log entries for the audit-driven scope expansion
Memory note: persist audit-finding categories so future audits can compare drift

human_actions_pending.action: Read 6 subsystem REVIEW.md files (summary aggregates; details matter)
human_actions_pending.context: Triage decisions can't be made from summary alone — per-subsystem context determines fix complexity

human_actions_pending.action: Decide which blockers gate v4.8 ship vs ship-with-documented-warnings
human_actions_pending.context: Memory: enterprise-first prioritization. HORIZON: v4.8 is primetime cutover (deploy-and-forget bar)

decisions.decision: Run audit BEFORE v4.8, not after
decisions.rationale: HORIZON.md frames v4.8 as primetime cutover with highest quality bar; audit at clean checkpoint maps findings to single state; audit findings inform v4.8 phase decomposition rather than disrupting it mid-flight
User explicit choice; primetime cutover spans entire codebase, not just newest surface

decisions.decision: 6 parallel gsd-code-reviewer agents at deep depth, partitioned by subsystem
decisions.rationale: gsd-code-review skill is phase-scoped; gsd-audit-fix is for UAT-debt only; direct parallel dispatch matches the 'wider audit' shape

decisions.decision: Proposed v4.8 split: Wave A (6 hardening phases 57-62) before Wave B (6 operating-model phases 63-68)
decisions.rationale: Audit revealed dashboard API has zero auth/CORS/rate-limiting; shipping scheduled+dashboard-launched scans on top of unhardened API would invert the primetime quality goal

decisions.decision: Excluded from audit: tests/, chaos lab, build/CI workflows, shadcn UI primitives
decisions.rationale: Scope discipline; each excluded surface is its own audit shape

next_action: Read .planning/audit-2026-05-08/AUDIT-SUMMARY.md, then walk the top 15 blockers with the user. Decide whether to commit audit artifacts as-is or refine first.
context_notes: v4.7 Governance & Compliance Platform shipped fully today (2026-05-08). Audit found 41+ blockers across 6 subsystems — bigger scope than HORIZON.md's pre-loaded 'residual trust/polish' track anticipated. The dashboard API hardening finding (zero auth/CORS/rate-limiting/CSRF on mutating routes) is the most v4.8-critical because v4.8 ships dashboard-initiated scans (BACK-86 slice 1). Recommend Wave A/B split before any v4.8 phase planning.
